title: ceropegia campanuliformis
intro: Ceropegia campanuliformis, also known as the 'twin flower' or 'twiner', is a flowering plant species in the Apocynaceae family, native to the tropical forests of Western Ghats in India. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Ceropegia campanuliformis include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an antioxidant. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of the plant are used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health ailments.
